---
title: "Minibatch OT"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.method-page}
# Minibatch Optimal Transport

In the baseline Flow Matching setup, the source noise samples and target residual patches can be paired independently.
Minibatch OT improves this by matching noise samples to target residuals with a lower transport cost:

$$
\pi^\star=\arg\min_{\pi}\sum_{i,j}\pi_{ij}\|\mathbf{x}_0^i-\mathbf{x}_1^j\|_2^2.
$$

In OceanLens, this is used in `v4_s1_logit_t` to make the training path less arbitrary and to expose the U-Net to more
coherent transitions between noise and ocean residuals.

Reference: [Optimal Transport Conditional Flow Matching](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.00482).
:::
